title: Avoiding re-render via key prop reset

performance_problem.symptom: Want to reset state via key
performance_problem.metric_impact: Track render_count_per_action: React Profiler shows only components whose inputs changed re-render.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Avoiding re-render via key prop reset regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

anti_patterns_to_avoid: Adding memoization before measuring renders
Moving state without proving ownership or invalidation boundaries
Hiding work behind Suspense or debounce while INP remains slow
reasoning: perf-181 treats Avoiding re-render via key prop reset as a react_rendering bottleneck. The fix is valid only when React Profiler, browser INP trace, render-count assertions, and interaction replay show render_count_per_action (count) meeting the row target: only components with changed inputs re-render; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
before_metrics: perf-181 baseline for Avoiding re-render via key prop reset: record render_count_per_action in count, sample at least five cold and five warm runs, keep the production-shaped fixture stable, and capture the user-visible symptom before changing code.
after_metrics: perf-181 optimized result for Avoiding re-render via key prop reset: render_count_per_action must meet target (only components with changed inputs re-render), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Avoiding re-render via key prop reset adds state-boundary and memoization discipline; keep the component model readable and remove the optimization if Profiler evidence disappears.
regression_prevention: React Profiler fixture for Avoiding re-render via key prop reset asserts render_count_per_action; browser INP budget and render-count snapshots fail the pull request on regression.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Avoiding re-render via key prop reset changes the nearest slow line without measuring render_count_per_action. It can move cost into another react_rendering boundary, hide cold-start or tail behavior, and leave the original user-visible symptom outside automated budgets.

title: Avoiding pg_dump on prod

performance_problem.metric_impact: Track primary_io_wait_ms: primary read/write latency unchanged while backup runs from a replica.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Avoiding pg_dump on prod regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

anti_patterns_to_avoid: Changing indexes or pools without EXPLAIN/pg_stat evidence
Optimizing the happy query while ignoring locks, replicas, and rollback
Letting ORM defaults hide query count or transaction scope
reasoning: perf-189 treats Avoiding pg_dump on prod as a database bottleneck. The fix is valid only when EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS), pg_stat_statements, lock/connection wait telemetry, and a rollback rehearsal show primary_io_wait_ms (milliseconds) meeting the row target: primary IO wait unchanged while backup runs off-primary; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
solution:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
: "${PG_REPLICA_URL:?replica required}"
: "${BACKUP_DIR:?backup dir required}"

pg_dump "$PG_REPLICA_URL"   --format=custom   --no-owner   --no-acl   --file="$BACKUP_DIR/app-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M).dump"

# Backups run from a replica or snapshot, never the primary during peak load; monitor replica lag and primary IO wait.
before_metrics: perf-189 baseline for Avoiding pg_dump on prod: record primary_io_wait_ms in milliseconds, sample at least five cold and five warm runs, keep the production-shaped fixture stable, and capture the user-visible symptom before changing code.
after_metrics: perf-189 optimized result for Avoiding pg_dump on prod: primary_io_wait_ms must meet target (primary IO wait unchanged while backup runs off-primary), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Avoiding pg_dump on prod may add index/storage/operational complexity; keep the old path or rollback migration until EXPLAIN and production metrics prove the database budget holds.
regression_prevention: CI stores an EXPLAIN/pg_stat snapshot for Avoiding pg_dump on prod; fail if primary_io_wait_ms regresses, row scans/locks increase, or the rollback migration is missing.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Avoiding pg_dump on prod changes the nearest slow line without measuring primary_io_wait_ms. It can move cost into another database boundary, hide cold-start or tail behavior, and leave the original user-visible symptom outside automated budgets.

anti_patterns_to_avoid: Reducing requests by weakening cache correctness
Retrying every failure without jitter or idempotency

reasoning: perf-197 treats Avoiding referrer policy issues as a network bottleneck. The fix is valid only when network waterfall replay, cache/preflight counters, retry logs, and server timing headers show critical_waterfall_ms (milliseconds) meeting the row target: critical request waterfall shortens without cache correctness loss; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
before_metrics: perf-197 baseline for Avoiding referrer policy issues: record critical_waterfall_ms in milliseconds, sample at least five cold and five warm runs, keep the production-shaped fixture stable, and capture the user-visible symptom before changing code.
after_metrics: perf-197 optimized result for Avoiding referrer policy issues: critical_waterfall_ms must meet target (critical request waterfall shortens without cache correctness loss), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Avoiding referrer policy issues can trade latency for cache staleness or server concurrency; document freshness bounds and backpressure limits.
regression_prevention: Waterfall replay for Avoiding referrer policy issues records critical_waterfall_ms, cache hit ratio, preflight count, and bounded retry behavior.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Avoiding referrer policy issues changes the nearest slow line without measuring critical_waterfall_ms.
